pages: Hello, unyu! I'm assuming you're an unyu.
Did you know? I am placing helpful signs everywhere to help Unyus survive the winter.
I heard they need to collect those fire orbs because they're so weak and frail and dumb.
No offense though.
Anyway, did you know?
You can spit liquid out by pressing B. The droplet will freeze shortly afterwards, creating a platform.
If you hold UP, the platform will be above you. Otherwise, it will be to the side.
Also, if you hold DOWN, the platform will be placed closer when placed to the side.
You can refill your liquid meter at a dispenser. They serve as respawn points as well.
If you ever get stuck, you can respawn to the last dispenser from the pause menu.
I hope this information was useful!
Hello, unyu!
Did you know? The water is dangerously cold!
This is because the environment is very cold. Did you know that?
It could be fatal for an Unyu. I don't know how that kills them though. It's not even that cold.
I hope this information was useful!
Did you know? There is a Blux up ahead. Oh no!
Don't ask how I knew that ahead of time. Too complicated.
Fortunately, you can shoot at it by switching to attack mode by pressing L.
Then, by pressing B, you will spit water bullets in the direction you are facing.
You can even shoot up and downwards if you're holding the respective directions down. Did you know that?
I hope this information was useful!
...Oh! I almost forgot to mention:
Your fire rate is unlimited. So you shoot as fast as you can mash the button.
I hope that information was useful as well!
Did you know? You can push objects!
Also, you can't place platforms on torches or sky holes.
I hope this information was useful!
Did you know? Sometimes I don't have useful information.
So, I try to instead explain how to get to the other side of a certain room.
Although, even that explanation turns out unfinished and useless.
In order to not waste your time reading useless information, I adopted a color scheme!
Lighter-colored signs, like this one, indicate a hint for a puzzle.
But the darker brown ones you should definitely read! You might die if you don't.
I hope this information is useful!
Did you know? Oh no! There's a wall here that's two feet tall!.
Fortunately, if you try really hard, you can jump over two-foot walls!
Isn't that great?
I hope this information was useful!
why do i have to write these stupid signs.
my brother died last week writing the sign in the other room.
truthfully he was always a bit of a dimwit, but he was
well. he used his last breath to ask me to continue his work.
that i needed to finish off writing the last of the signs.
it's really dangerous around these parts though. and i don't think these signs help make it much safer.
but i want him to rest easy in the afterlife.
You can't leave yet! You need to collect four red fire orbs.
Did you know? I'm a world-renowned writer,
with prose that matches the likes of William Shakespeare and and Anton Chekhov.
Here is a poem I made:
In that lake of sparkling blue / In that large and spacious roo- /
m, There is more than meets the eyes. / Obscured by darkness, its spaciousness hides. /
This poem's quality will make you cry / of laughter. Not of sad cry.
Yay!!
Yikes. This nook here is pretty dangerous!
Well, for an Unyu. I can just shoot that bottom Blux from here.
But Unyus can't do that for whatever reason. I don't get it. How is it hard???
Anyway, here's some advice. You should formulate a plan first.
Observe the paths the projectiles take to find safe areas.
Then you should be able to kill both of them without getting hit.
I hope this information is useful!
Did you know? There's a platform up there! I really wonder what's on it.
But I can't bring that spring over here! There's this dumb pit!
If only there was a way I could make a bridge over it...
I hope this information was useful! it probably isn't though :(
Did you know? If you jump up into a moveable block above you, the block will get bumped upwards.
It's called the Law of Conservation, Newton's 7th Law. I think. Something about a cradle...
I dunno. I'm not a physician.
Actually I don't think this information is very useful...
Also if anyone knows how to get up that wall please tell me.
Did you know? Pushable objects can bounce on springs too, not just you!
Nevermind. Obviously!
Why would only living beings be able to bounce on springs?
It's not like this is a game.
Ugh, sorry. I don't have any useful information. What a waste of wood.
Obscured by view resides a tiny room
Across the pond. Approach by shooting ice.
Within this alcove lies a little shroom.
I recommend to fire upwards thrice.
Revealed to you will be a yellow 'key'.
Did you know? There's an ice block on the ceiling to the left.
Although, I don't know how to get it.
Oh! Did you know? There's an enemy below this platform!
It holds up the ice block, and...
Nevermind, I don't think I have any useful information. Sorry.
Well, if you get truly stuck, you can reset the room by leaving and re-entering it.
Oh, well, you probably already read the sign that told you that. Nevermind.
Beware the enemies! They shoot corrosive black goop everywhere.
You could get seriously injured if one of their projectiles hit you!
Did you know? You can shoot back at them. If you want.
I hope this information was useful!
Did you know? Your dispenser checkpoints save the state of the room at the time you triggered it.
To go back, select "RESPAWN" from the pause menu.
And if you want to reset the room completely, you do so by leaving and re-entering it.
This cave is really weird sometimes.
I hope this information was useful!
Did you know?
I'm a sign!
I hope this information was useful!
Did you know?
Statistically, 60.59% of the people who read this sign found it useful.
I hope this information is useful!
Did you know? You can shoot these ceiling-mounted enemies from below.
Just quickly go under them, shoot upwards, move out of the way, and repeat until they're down!
I hope this infor
The winters of this world are extraordinarily cold.
During the winter months, Unyus migrate to live within the caverns of The Big Mountain.
But that alone is not sufficient for survival.
Fire Orbs accumulate inside the caverns during the summer.
These mysterious, elusive objects provide a long-lasting and versatile source of heat.
In order for Unyus to survive the winters, they must explore the caverns to find them.
But this is no easy task. Other, more dangerous creatures live in these caverns.
And, unfortunately, Unyus taste yummy. Muahaha...
